title: Houston Prom 2026: Complete Parent's Guide to Safe Prom Limo Service
summary: Complete guide for Houston parents booking prom limo service. Safety tips, pricing, timeline, and vehicle recommendations for prom night 2026.
author: Adam - AvaLimo
content:
Prom is one of the biggest nights of your teen's high school experience. As a parent, you want them to have an unforgettable time — but safety comes first. Here's everything you need to know about booking a safe, reliable prom limo in Houston.

Why a Professional Limo Beats DIY Transport

Every prom season, too many teens pile into a friend's car or try to coordinate multiple rideshares. A professional limo service eliminates the risks:

  • Licensed, insured, background-checked chauffeur
  • No distracted driving — your teen enjoys the night
  • Guaranteed arrival — no cancellations, no wait times
  • One vehicle for the whole group — everyone stays together
  • Flat-rate pricing — no surge fees on prom night

Prom Night Timeline

4:00 PM — Chauffeur arrives at first pickup
4:15 PM — Group photos at a scenic Houston location
5:00 PM — Arrive at prom venue
10:00 PM — Pickup from prom
12:00 AM — Curfew drop-offs

Popular prom vehicles include our Mercedes Sprinter (up to 14) for large groups and the Cadillac Escalade (up to 6) for smaller parties. Book at least 3-4 weeks ahead — prom season fills fast.

Ready to book? Reserve your prom limo online or call (832) 567-8050.

title: How Much Does Wedding Limo Service Cost in Houston? (2026 Pricing Guide)

author: Adam - AvaLimo
content:
One of the most common questions we hear from Houston couples is: "How much should I budget for wedding transportation?" Here's a complete breakdown of wedding limo costs in Houston for 2026.

Average Wedding Transportation Costs

  • Mercedes S-Class (couple) — $100/hr, 3-hour minimum
  • Cadillac Escalade (wedding party up to 6) — $100/hr, 3-hour minimum
  • Mercedes Sprinter (guest shuttle up to 14) — $100/hr, 4-hour minimum

Most wedding packages range from $350 to $1,500 depending on vehicle count and duration. Essential Package (1 vehicle, 3 hrs): $350+. Premium Package (2 vehicles, 5 hrs): $750+. Ultimate Package (3+ vehicles, 8 hrs): $1,200+.

Tips to Save

  • Book 2-3 months ahead for best vehicle selection
  • Bundle the couple's car with a guest shuttle for better rates
  • Choose a flat-rate company to avoid hidden fees

Get your wedding transportation quote or call (832) 567-8050.

content:

title: IAH vs Hobby: Which Houston Airport Should You Fly Into?
summary: A complete guide to Houston's two airports — amenities, locations, and which one makes sense for your trip.
content:
Houston is served by two major airports: George Bush Intercontinental (IAH) and William P. Hobby (HOU). Choosing the right one can save you time, money, and stress.

George Bush Intercontinental (IAH) is Houston's primary international airport, located about 23 miles north of downtown. It has five terminals and serves most major international carriers. IAH is the better choice for international flights, long-haul domestic routes, and if you're traveling to or from the north side of Houston (The Woodlands, Spring, Conroe).

William P. Hobby (HOU) is smaller, closer to downtown (about 7 miles), and primarily serves domestic routes via Southwest Airlines and a few other carriers. It's much easier to navigate with shorter security lines and quicker baggage claim. Hobby is ideal for short-haul domestic flights and if you're staying in downtown, Midtown, or the Medical Center.

Our recommendation: For international travel, fly into IAH. For domestic trips, especially with Southwest, Hobby is hard to beat. Either way, AvaLimo covers both airports with the same premium service — flight tracking included.

title: How Early Should You Arrive at IAH? A Complete Airport Timeline
summary: Everything you need to know about timing your arrival at Houston's busiest airport.
content:
Timing your airport arrival can make or break your travel experience. Here's the complete guide for IAH.

Recommended Arrival Times:

  • Domestic flights: 2 hours before departure
  • International flights: 3 hours before departure
  • Peak times (holidays, summer): Add 30-60 minutes

Terminal Guide:

  • Terminal A: United domestic — typically fastest security
  • Terminal B: Southwest, Spirit — moderate wait times
  • Terminal C: United main hub — allow extra time
  • Terminal D: International arrivals — customs can take 30-90 min
  • Terminal E: Mixed international — varies by time of day

With AvaLimo: We track your flight and adjust pickup time automatically. No stress about traffic or timing — just show up and fly.
